task_definition.title: Warm-start protocol with LRU pool
task_definition.description: Implement warm-start protocol for resuming evaluation from cached intermediate states. Includes OpaqueState type, WarmStartable trait, WarmStatePool with LRU eviction and budget enforcement, and CacheStore integration via donate/retrieve helpers.

plan.title: Warm-start protocol with LRU pool
plan.analysis:
This task introduces a warm-start protocol to resume evaluation from cached intermediate states, enabling faster re-evaluation (e.g., solver warm-starts, kernel state reuse). The implementation spans three crates:

1. **reify-types** (foundation): New `warm` module with `OpaqueState` (type-erased container wrapping `Box<dyn Any + Send>` with estimated_size tracking) and `WarmStartable` trait (`warm_state()` / `with_warm_state()`).

2. **reify-runtime** (pool): New `warm_pool` module with `WarmStatePool` providing LRU eviction and budget enforcement. Uses `HashMap<NodeId, PoolEntry>` keyed by NodeId from reify-eval, with `Instant`-based access tracking.

3. **reify-eval** (integration): Extend `NodeCache` with an `Option<OpaqueState>` field, add `donate_warm_state()` and `get_warm_state()` helpers on `CacheStore` with take semantics.

Key design constraints: OpaqueState must be Send (for concurrent eval), warm state is transient (not cloned/serialized), pool uses LRU eviction when budget exceeded, retrieve uses take semantics (state removed from pool on retrieve). No journal.rs exists yet in this worktree so WarmStartUsed events are out of scope.

Existing patterns followed: saturating arithmetic for size tracking, `#[cfg(test)]` inline test modules, mock-based trait testing, `NodeId` as the universal cache key, DependencyTrace default for simple entries.

steps.description: Write tests for OpaqueState in reify-types/src/warm.rs: construction with new<T>(), estimated_size_bytes() accessor, downcast<T>() roundtrip (success + wrong-type returns None), downcast_ref<T>() (success + wrong-type), Send bound verification via compile-time assert

Implement OpaqueState struct in reify-types/src/warm.rs: inner: Box<dyn Any + Send>, estimated_size: usize. Methods: new<T: Any + Send>(value, estimated_size_bytes), estimated_size_bytes(), downcast<T: Any>(self) -> Option<T>, downcast_ref<T: Any>(&self) -> Option<&T>. Add 'pub mod warm;' and re-exports to reify-types/src/lib.rs

Write tests for WarmStartable trait in reify-types/src/warm.rs: define a MockWarmStartable struct implementing the trait, test warm_state() returns Some with correct data, test with_warm_state() restores state, test with_warm_state() with wrong type silently ignores, test warm_state() returns None when no state stored

Implement WarmStartable trait in reify-types/src/warm.rs: warm_state(&self) -> Option<OpaqueState>, with_warm_state(&mut self, state: OpaqueState). Best-effort semantics — implementors silently ignore wrong-type state

Write tests for WarmStatePool in reify-runtime/src/warm_pool.rs: new(budget_bytes) sets budget, donate + retrieve roundtrip returns correct state, retrieve uses take semantics (second retrieve returns None), used_bytes tracks correctly after donate/retrieve, budget_bytes accessor, clear empties pool, len tracks entry count

Implement WarmStatePool struct in reify-runtime/src/warm_pool.rs with basic storage: pool: HashMap<NodeId, PoolEntry>, budget_bytes, used_bytes. PoolEntry: { state: OpaqueState, last_accessed: Instant, size_bytes: usize }. Methods: new(), donate(), retrieve() with take semantics, used_bytes(), budget_bytes(), clear(), len(). Add 'pub mod warm_pool;' to reify-runtime/src/lib.rs

Write LRU eviction tests for WarmStatePool: donate exceeding budget evicts least-recently-accessed entry, access ordering updated on retrieve+re-donate, eviction loop continues until item fits or only one remains, single oversized item allowed in empty pool, zero-budget pool rejects all items, same-node re-donate replaces existing entry and updates used_bytes

Implement LRU eviction in WarmStatePool.donate(): when used_bytes + new_size > budget_bytes, find entry with oldest last_accessed, evict it (subtract from used_bytes), repeat until fits or pool has <= 1 entry. Handle same-node replacement by subtracting old size first. Use saturating_sub for safety

Write tests for CacheStore warm state integration in reify-eval/src/cache.rs: NodeCache with warm_state field (defaults to None), donate_warm_state() stores on existing node (returns true), donate_warm_state() on missing node returns false, get_warm_state() retrieves and removes (take semantics), get_warm_state() returns None on second call, warm_state is None after record_evaluation(), warm_state is NOT preserved across NodeCache clone

Extend NodeCache with pub warm_state: Option<OpaqueState> field (default None). Update NodeCache::new() to set warm_state: None. Add donate_warm_state(&mut self, node: &NodeId, state: OpaqueState) -> bool and get_warm_state(&mut self, node: &NodeId) -> Option<OpaqueState> to CacheStore. Ensure record_evaluation() clears warm_state. Implement custom Clone for NodeCache that sets warm_state to None (transient). Update reify-types OpaqueState to implement Debug manually

design_decisions.decision: OpaqueState uses Box<dyn Any + Send> for type erasure
design_decisions.rationale: Evaluators have heterogeneous state types (solver matrices, kernel handles, etc.). Type erasure via Any lets any evaluator stash arbitrary state without the pool knowing concrete types. Send bound required for concurrent scheduler (Arc<E> + tokio::spawn).

design_decisions.decision: retrieve() uses take semantics (removes state from pool/cache on access)
design_decisions.rationale: Warm state is consumed during evaluation — the evaluator takes ownership to restore internal state. Leaving stale state in the pool would waste budget. Single-use semantics simplify reasoning about ownership.

design_decisions.decision: Warm state is transient — NOT preserved across Clone
design_decisions.rationale: OpaqueState wraps Box<dyn Any + Send> which is not Clone. Snapshot cloning (O(1) via PersistentMap) must not be blocked by non-Clone warm state. Warm state is a performance optimization, not semantic state — safe to drop on clone.

design_decisions.decision: LRU eviction uses Instant::now() for access tracking, not a logical clock
design_decisions.rationale: Wall-clock time is sufficient for LRU ordering in a single-process evaluator. Avoids adding a counter/clock abstraction. Consistent with the existing runtime's use of tokio (real-time system).

design_decisions.decision: WarmStatePool lives in reify-runtime, not reify-eval
design_decisions.rationale: The pool is a scheduling-layer concern — the runtime decides when to donate/retrieve warm state around evaluation. CacheStore in reify-eval only stores warm state per-node as a convenience; the pool manages the global budget. reify-runtime already depends on reify-eval, so it can use NodeId.

design_decisions.decision: Zero-budget pool rejects all donations rather than panicking
design_decisions.rationale: Graceful degradation — a zero budget means warm-start is disabled. No need for a separate disabled flag; the budget naturally prevents any storage.

design_decisions.decision: Custom Debug impl for OpaqueState rather than derive
design_decisions.rationale: Box<dyn Any + Send> does not implement Debug. A manual impl that shows the estimated_size_bytes and type_id provides useful diagnostics without requiring inner types to be Debug.

design_decisions.decision: No journal/event support in this task
design_decisions.rationale: journal.rs does not exist in this worktree. WarmStartUsed event logging would be a separate task. This task focuses solely on the data structures and pool mechanics.

reuse.how: Import reify_eval::cache::NodeId as the key for the pool HashMap. Consistent with CacheStore which already uses NodeId.
Follow the existing CacheStore API patterns: HashMap<NodeId, _>, get/put/invalidate style, take semantics matching the existing cache clear_dirty pattern.
Use saturating_sub when decrementing used_bytes to prevent underflow, consistent with safe arithmetic patterns in the codebase.

reuse.what: Mock-based testing pattern from runtime tests
reuse.how: Define inline MockWarmStartable struct in #[cfg(test)] module, implementing the trait with an Option<OpaqueState> field. Mirrors the MockAsyncEvaluator pattern in concurrent.rs tests.
Use ValueCellId::new('Entity', 'member') and NodeId::Value(...) for creating test node IDs, consistent with existing test patterns across reify-eval and reify-runtime.
Use default traces when creating NodeCache entries in tests, matching the pattern in existing cache.rs tests.
